title: Claytonia barneoudii
taxonomy: Claytonia barneoudii is classified as a species within the genus Claytonia and the family Portulacaceae. Claytonia barneoudii belongs to the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is further classified as a member of the phylum Magnoliophyta and the kingdom Plantae. This species is native to the Andes mountain range in South America. Claytonia barneoudii is also known as a rare and endangered species due to its limited geographic distribution.

The plant is prized for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in addressing various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections.
